clinical_coherence.notes: 71M East Asian with classic right-sided PD: resting tremor, cogwheel rigidity, bradykinesia with decrement, micrographia, shuffling gait with reduced arm swing. Vitals reasonable. Neuro exam detailed and internally consistent. Hyposmia and constipation add non-motor features.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Normal MRI expected for PD. Labs normal except mild hypernatremia and borderline low Hgb. DaTscan shows asymmetric left putaminal loss contralateral to right-sided symptoms - correct lateralization. Robust 50% levodopa response. Autonomic testing normal (argues against MSA). PSG confirms subclinical RBD with 18% RWA index.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G20 correct. Well-calibrated differential including ET, drug-induced, MSA, PSP, NPH. Critical and contraindicated actions clinically sound. Mother's possible undiagnosed tremor noted as potential risk factor.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic textbook PD with asymmetric onset, pill-rolling tremor, hyposmia, and robust levodopa response. Perfectly straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 71-year-old East Asian male with HTN, DM2, BPH, chronic back pain. Highly realistic for PD onset in this age group.

educational_value.notes: Solid standard PD case. Adds hyposmia and constipation as prodromal features which enhances educational value. Comprehensive workup.

strengths: Excellent lateralization consistency between exam and DaTscan
